mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by less than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive monthly decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month nonfarm payroll employment growth reflects sustained economic expansion, reinforcing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes versus 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 220.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth validate top-down and bottom-up demand signals, confirming earnings momentum and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 60% of aligned observations during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns (accuracy < 52%) over a 4-year walk-forward test.
